kept.text:
I'm moving on from @Shopify.

When I joined 7.5 years ago, friends were literally taking bets on whether I'd last a year. The idea of me thriving inside a large company (and Shopify was a lot smaller back then!) felt unlikely. Most people assumed I'd be itching to start my next thing.

Instead, Shopify became my company.

I wasn't a founder, but @tobi always made me feel like one. It wasn't a startup, but Shopify moves like one. And the mission isn't just words — it shows up in the decisions, every day.

I got to wear a lot of hats. Introducing Shop to the world. Going deep on the rails of how money moves on the internet. Reimagining Shopify for the age of AI through design.

Leaving is hard. Shopify changed my life in more ways than I can recount. It taught me how to build for the long term, gave me a bar for craft and ambition I'll carry into everything that comes next, and showed me what it looks like when a company lives its mission.

In my next chapter, I'm getting the opportunity to bring together all the things I've obsessed over the past decade — consumer mobile, finance and money movement,  design, AI — into a single role. More on that soon.

profile_bio.description: CDO at @Shopify, reimagining the platform for the age of AI. Previously launched and led @shop and other things. Talk about how to build really great products

kept.text:
時給2ドルのリトアニア人が、たった1年でShopifyアプリを月1500万円→月1.5億円（10倍）に成長させた天才Erikas氏、えぐい

リトアニア出身の彼は、時給2ドルのWeb代理店勤務からフリーランスを経て、Shopifyアプリ開発へ転身。

彼がゼロから月1.5億円に到達した方法：

❶ FBグループ・Discordに「まだ作ってないアプリ」のデザイン案を投稿し需要を事前検証
❷ バンドル販売アプリ「Kaching」をローンチ。売上連動価格でEC事業者とWin-Winの仕組み
❸ 4つのアプリを「Kaching」ブランドで展開、利益率90%超え・年20億円規模へ

「新しいアイデアを探す」より「既存競合にどう勝つか」を問うのが彼の哲学。狙う市場を変えるだけで収益が桁違いになるという事例ですよね。

詳しくはこちら 👇
https://t.co/MoHhkSV6f1

kept.text:
brands are getting shut down by Shopify payments at 0.1% chargeback rates, even if you have clean metrics, a stable business, and zero warning. If they decide your business is too risky, they will pull the plug.
 
here's what the payment stack should look like as you scale, built from the bottom up:
 
↳ tier 1, up to $10M/year - Shopify payments or Stripe (same underlying platform, Stripe powers Shopify payments).
 
works great until you skew a risk ratio, your supplier ships a product one or two weeks late, or you get a few disputes on quality. then it's a reserve, and a shutdown two or three weeks later with zero warning.
 
↳ tier 1 shutdown triggers - a supplement they no longer want to support, CBD or THC, subscriptions they don't want to support anymore, or anything they decide is too risky.
 
↳ tier 2, high-risk processing - where most $0-$10M/month operators land after a ban. fees are a little more expensive than shopify but the risk tolerances are higher and they support more products and business models.
 
↳ tier 3, past $10M/year - now you're a bigger fish and you can walk into the higher channels with leverage.
 
"i've been on high-risk processing for 6 months, account's stable, no big reserves, no chargebacks, $10M/month brand."
 
that unlocks payment facilitators like Adyen, Airwallex, and Stripe. cheaper rates similar to shopify, higher approvals, and more stable. below $10-15M/year in annual revenue, that door is just closed off to you.
 
↳ tier 4, $50M-$100M/year - building on Adyen and Airwallex as the main channels. still keeping the high-risk mids in the stack for any payments that decline or transactions they don't want sending to the main processors.
 
those route to the high-risk mids as a fallback.
 
when a brand gets shut down with zero warning, there are really only two options. 

tweet it out and hope somebody helps or Shopify magically reinstates you, or move to high-risk processing and start building the stack properly.

quoted_tweet.text:
【Shopify AI Toolkit】
検証用の開発ストアにテーマを移行する際、テーマコードのコピー自体はファイルのエクスポート／インポートやGitHub連携で済むのですが、テーマエディタ上で登録されたバナー画像や商品動画といったコンテンツファイルが引き継がれないため、インストール直後はストアの見た目が崩れた状態になる——この問題がずっと悩みでした。

Shopifyのテーマではassetsディレクトリに入れたファイルでない限り、同じファイル名のファイルが移行先の「ファイル＞コンテンツ」にも存在していないと、画像などが一切表示されません。

そこで、Matrixifyを使って数百〜数千ファイルを手動でアップロードし直すのもなかなか手間なので、Shopify AI Toolkitを使って以下の仕組みで自動化してみたところ、これが非常にうまくいきました。

① テーマファイルを検証用開発ストアに従来の手順でインストール（この時点では画像リンクは壊れた状態）
② 本番ストアのファイル一覧をAI Toolkitでエクスポートし、CDN URLを取得
③ AI Toolkitで開発ストアにファイルを取り込む（CDNを直接参照し、元のファイル名のまま登録）

ポイントは「ダウンロードして再アップロード」ではなく、URLを渡すだけでファイルの取り込みを完結してくれる点です。ファイル名も保持されるので、テーマ側の参照を一切変更せず解決してくれます。

数千件規模のファイルであっても途中停止・再開に対応しており、本番環境と見た目がほぼ完全に一致する検証用ストアをスムーズに構築できるようになりました。

#Shopify #ShopifyAIToolkit
